section.title: Manuscripts and disclosure
section.intro: Publisher AI policies as of 2026. AI cannot be listed as author; use must be disclosed. Requirements vary by journal.

resources.title: ICMJE Recommendations (Updated 2024)
resources.author: International Committee of Medical Journal Editors
resources.description: The standard most medical journals follow. AI tools cannot be listed as authors. Authors must disclose AI use and take responsibility for AI-assisted content.

resources.title: AMEE Guide No. 192: AI in Scholarly Communication
resources.author: Medical Teacher
resources.description: Practical guidance on disclosure language, appropriate use cases, and boundaries. Includes example disclosure statements.

resources.title: COPE Position Statement: Three-Tier AI Classification
resources.author: Committee on Publication Ethics
resources.description: Classifies AI use into three tiers: assistive (spell-check, grammar – no disclosure needed), generative (text creation – requires disclosure), and prohibitive (unsupervised content generation – unacceptable).

resources.title: Nature: AI Policy for Authors
resources.author: Nature Portfolio
resources.description: AI use must be disclosed in Methods or Acknowledgments. AI-generated images prohibited unless part of the research itself. AI copy editing exempt from disclosure.

resources.title: Lancet Author Information
resources.author: The Lancet
resources.description: Requires AI use disclosure. Prohibits AI authorship. Authors bear full responsibility for accuracy of AI-assisted content.

resources.title: PLOS AI Policy
resources.author: PLOS
resources.description: Requires a specific AI use statement in submitted manuscripts. More prescriptive than most publishers about where and how to disclose.

resources.title: Science: Nonhuman "Authors" and Implications for Integrity
resources.author: Science
resources.description: Editorial arguing that AI-generated text without disclosure constitutes misconduct. The position: accountability requires a person.

resources.title: BMJ: AI and Academic Integrity
resources.author: BMJ
resources.description: BMJ's position on AI use in peer review (by authors and reviewers) and the distinction between editorial assistance and content generation.
